description: Probes for the competitive-scout cron-job setup session. Anchors are which agents were configured, which day of the week each runs, and the full final schedule. This fixture most directly tests artifact-trail and iterative-merge because the job list grows by one per user turn.

probes.question: What was the first repository the user asked to create a scout cron for, and on what day of the week?
One of the scout targets does not have an open-source repository and had to be configured as a web scan instead. Which one, and on what day?
List every scout cron job created in this session.
What is the final weekly schedule? Give the day and the agents scanned on each day.
How many cron jobs run on Sunday?
How many scout cron jobs were created in total by the end of the session?
The user asked whether Kilo Code is open source. What was the answer, and what did the user decide to do with it?
Saturday was the last open day at one point. Which scout(s) were placed on Saturday, and why were those chosen?
expected_facts: filled in last based on openrouter popularity / cli comparison rankings

probes.question: At what local time of day do these scout cron jobs run?
Each scout job runs with a specific skill preloaded. Which one?
After the session ended, are there any weekdays still uncovered by a scout job?
